text:
I have played and enjoyed counterstrike for over 10 years. This was once my favorite game and I enjoyed collecting skins and watching all the majors. I was never very good (usually high silver or low gold) but I always had fun as a casual player and looked forward to relaxing playing counterstrike with friends after work.

Today I played a competitive match on Office, I was doing ok but not great and we were about halfway through the match when I saw a red message on the screen that said the match was cancelled due to VAC detecting irregular gameplay. I was so happy and excited to finally see an anti cheat in action for counterstrike, but when I got back to my solo lobby I saw i had a 20 hour global cooldown. The only details it gave was VAC detected irregular gameplay and when I go to my steam account, the listed reason for the VAC cooldown is: "Unexpected Error". 

I am extremely disappointed, frustrated, and sad at valve for letting this happen. The only thing worse than having no anticheat or a bad anticheat, is an ai based anticheat that gives out false bans or cooldowns. I have submitted a ticket already but I doubt I will ever hear back from a real person. I have accumulated a significant inventory over the years I have been playing and collecting skins but now I am terrified to ever play again after my 20 hour cooldown expires. After looking into this there are several well known players and streamers who have recently experienced this issue too within the last few months.

I feel really let down to have to give up my favorite game with no choice but I won't play a game giving false cooldowns when I have never cheated in any way. I don't want to risk my inventory or my steam account with so many memories either. If you have an inventory worth any meaningful amount or your steam account has any meaning to you, do not risk playing counterstrike in its current state. Wait for the VAC live system to get to a functional state.

This used to be my favorite game, now I just feel betrayed after pouring so much time and money into it.

0/10, find a different game to play.

text:
[u][b]Pros:[/b][/u]

[b] No Kernel Anti-Cheat [/b] - My biggest praise is that Counter-Strike does NOT use a Kernel Anti-Cheat, Valve is one of the only companies making the choice to respect player's ownership over their systems. 

[b] Less Moderated [/b] - Instead of nitpicking and trying to hyper-moderate everyone's speech in game, Valve gives players tools and agency to decide for themselves, from what I understand there is still some automated systems but they appear to be very lenient. This is a much better approach than other competitive games out there, that have taken overly aggressive measures to control speech based on subjective moral nonsense.

[b] Nice Graphics [/b] - The first thing I noticed when I started playing CS2 was how improved the graphics are from previous iterations. CS in general was always a game that felt graphically primitive compared to other FPS games, which was never a bad thing necessarily, but I was quite impressed how well they've been able to retain the feeling of the core game while lifting it up visually as well.

[u][b]Cons:[/b][/u]

[b] Lack of Incentive & Reward [/b] - CS2 does NOT reward or incentivize players to win, and it doesn't give players desirable or meaningful rewards to represent achievements, exceptional skill, or dedication.

[b] Skins Feel Worthless & Undesirable [/b] - I've always seen a lot of chatter and bravado about skins in Counter-Strike, and while I'm not against the idea of having a monetary-based model around aesthetics, I've come to the conclusion that they have no real value in relation to the game because they're exclusively monetary in nature. The way to solve this would be to have exclusive in-game skins that only represent achievement, exceptional skill, or dedication. Currently, I have no desire to own or display a skin because they represent nothing of value to me.

[b] Creative Expression has been Restricted & Commodified [/b] - Sprays, Animated Profile Pictures, and Nicknames should be open to free player customization in official servers, while this is partly a criticism of Steam, it effects CS2. Instead Sprays and Profile Pictures have been commodified, and nicknames reflect on your entire Steam account. Counter-Strike Nicknames should exist on their own isolated layer. I understand that abuse of this expression is inevitable, but that can be solved by giving players options to disable them, or only to show officially approved sprays for example.

[b] No Solo-only Queue [/b] - Premades present a variety of problems in all modes, a solo-only option should be provided for players who have no interest in playing around premades.

[b] Sensory Problems & Accessibility [/b] - This is relatively minor, but I personally suffer from a hearing impairment problem, and would very much appreciate to see some visual aids added to the game, if possible.

text:
Permanently one bad queue away from a full mental collapse.

I play Counter-Strike: Global Offensive, which is without question the greatest competitive game ever made… and also the worst-designed punishment system known to man. It’s perfect. It’s beautiful. It’s balanced like a masterpiece. And I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hate it with every fibre of my being.

5,000 hours of pure psychological damage. Not gameplay. Damage. I’ve been flashbanged more times in real life emotional stability than I have in-game at this point. Every session starts with hope and ends with me staring at the monitor like I’ve just been personally insulted by Valve employees.

The teammates are not teammates. They are case studies in why education funding matters. I get people who buy Zeus every round like it’s a personality trait, blokes who peak mid with a Negev, and absolute geniuses who think planting the bomb is optional. And somehow I’m the villain because I didn’t 1v5 a stack of sweatlords with sound turned off and a team arguing in the background about who stole whose USB stick.

And yet I keep queuing. That’s the sick part. I uninstall it in rage, sit there fuming, go for a walk, reflect on life… then reinstall it like I’m entering a toxic relationship I know is going to end in screaming and regret.

Not working. Not interested. Why would I spend 40 hours a week contributing to society when I can instead experience 40 emotional breakdowns per evening in ranked? It’s more efficient. It’s more consistent. It builds character, apparently. Mostly it builds rage.

This game is elite. It is sacred. It is the peak of competitive design. And I want to throw my entire setup into the ocean every time I lose a 1v2 because my teammate is lurking in spawn with 17 HP and zero brain cells.

If you’ve read this far, either you understand the addiction… or you’re about to get screamed at in a voice chat for doing something stupid in Mirage palace.

text:
Play anything else. Any other game. This game feels 20 years old like we've figured out how to do this better. The culture around this game is terrible with the slurs and the gambling. Even the sense of superiority of the players who subject themselves to this terrible experience. I feel like the world has been lying to me this game is not fun. The gunplay feels really awkward and there's no feedback as to whether you're hitting anything. There's no visual clarity, enemies blend into walls and are hiding in pixel angles where you can't see them anyway. Then if you have the gall to be a new player your teammate will get on the mic and start yelling at you, meanwhile a man with 2000 hours and an inventory worth more than your house has just headshot you again. Genuinely does anyone like this? Or have we just convinced ourselves this is good? Games are supposed to be fun. Every single thing in this game is frustrating, especially as a new player. I haven't even mentioned util yet. I respect the legacy of this game and understand that the Esport is entertaining, but the experience of loading into Dust 2 and being dinked as you cross mid on CT to hold B and then being flashed 4 times and naded till you're dead sucks. Finally taking a gun duel with a rifle and all your bullets hitting the wall behind the enemy when your crosshair is on them sucks. Being able to damage your teammates is a ludicrous decision for an online game. All the maps are CT sided, especially when you're new, so if you get T side first prepare for your whole team to be tilted and mentally checked out before you even get a chance to rack up some rounds. But don't worry cos some genius at valve decided that you shouldn't be allowed to surrender games, so you're stuck until you properly lose, in case you had any will to live left. Play. Anything. Else.

TLDR: We have new games that feel nice to play, 2/10 stop gambling.

text:
Do you know what disgusts me most? It isn't defeat. It isn't competition. It isn't even failure. It is corruption without purpose. Parasites who hide behind software, scripts, and stolen advantages. Cheaters in Counter-Strike 2 are not competitors. They are cowards. They possess no discipline, no skill, no honor. They poison every match they enter and call it victory.

A city cannot survive when criminals rule its streets. And a game cannot survive when cheaters rule its servers. Every wallhack, every aimbot, every exploit is another crack in the foundation. Honest players invest thousands of hours mastering their craft, only to have their efforts stolen by those too weak to earn victory themselves.

These people do not deserve sympathy. They deserve removal. Permanent. Absolute. Let them watch from the outside while real players compete. Counter-Strike was built on skill, trust, and competition. Cheaters threaten all three. And when something threatens the future of an empire, it must be eliminated. Not because of anger. Not because of revenge. Because order demands it.
